entries.surface: packages/neko-agent/packages/extension/src/services/projectSearch/*
entries.replacement: @neko/search/host-vscode and @neko/search/core

entries.surface: legacy centralized domain-tool registration path
entries.replacement: DocumentReadCapabilityProvider, MediaReadCapabilityProvider, SemanticCoverageCapabilityProvider, and Agent-owned plugin skill discovery meta-tool.
entries.removeAfter: Removed by eliminate-legacy-fallback-surfaces; READ_DOCUMENT, READ_IMAGE, READ_DOCUMENT_IMAGE, and QUERY_SEMANTIC_COVERAGE now register through owner capability providers instead of toolBootstrap.
entries.notes: toolBootstrap may keep Agent-owned plugin skill catalogue discovery; domain document/media/search tools must use capability providers.

entries.surface: packages/neko-agent/packages/ai-sdk/src/bridge/*
entries.replacement: Explicit native media providers or provider-owned MediaAdapter execution selected without fallback
entries.removeAfter: Removed by adopt-pi-agent-runtime after media execution split into mutually exclusive native-provider and owning-adapter paths.
tests: pnpm --dir packages/neko-agent exec vitest run packages/ai-sdk/src/resolve.test.ts

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('fal', config, FalMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. No native fal.ai AI SDK path yet; provider can alternatively be configured as newapi/generic when using an OpenAI-compatible proxy.
migrationConditions: Native fal queue provider or documented newapi/generic compatible configuration covers text-to-image, image-to-image, ControlNet, IP-Adapter, and edit fields.
Auth tests cover fal.ai Key authentication and queue task ids.
Failure, cancellation, external task recovery, and result normalization tests pass without FalMediaAdapter bridge execution.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'fal' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after supported image tasks no longer require FalMediaAdapter.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('dashscope', config, DashScopeMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. No native DashScope AI SDK path yet.
migrationConditions: Native or compatible provider path covers Qwen image and Wan video requests.
Async task status polling, result download, retry/failure semantics, cancellation, and result normalization are covered by tests.
DashScopeMediaAdapter bridge is not used by default new-path acceptance tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'dashscope' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after supported media tasks run through native/generic providers.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('runway', config, RunwayMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Runway async video API needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native Runway provider covers text/image/video-to-video request fields, polling, cancellation, and result download.
Provider option and failure normalization tests pass without RunwayMediaAdapter bridge execution.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'runway' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Runway path covers supported video tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('luma', config, LumaMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Luma video API needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native Luma provider covers prompt/reference image requests, polling, cancellation, and result normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'luma' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Luma path covers supported video tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('suno', config, SunoMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Suno music/audio generation needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native Suno provider covers music prompt fields, wait/polling semantics, failure normalization, and audio output normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'suno' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Suno path covers supported audio/music tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('vidu', config, ViduMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Vidu video API needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native Vidu provider covers text/image-to-video, polling, cancellation, and result normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'vidu' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Vidu path covers supported video tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('midjourney', config, MidjourneyMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Midjourney proxy APIs are not OpenAI-compatible enough for the generic path.
migrationConditions: Native Midjourney provider covers imagine/status/result flow and failure normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'midjourney' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Midjourney path covers supported image tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('minimax', config, MiniMaxMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. MiniMax video API needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native MiniMax provider covers video request fields, polling, cancellation, and result normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'minimax' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native MiniMax path covers supported video tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('liblib', config, LiblibMediaAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createLegacyBridgeProvider()
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Temporary migration bridge only. Liblib image/workflow API needs provider-specific native support.
migrationConditions: Native Liblib provider covers image/workflow request fields, polling, cancellation, and result normalization.
Bridge execution is poisoned in native-path tests.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Remove providerType 'liblib' from AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES after native Liblib path covers supported image tasks.

sunsetProviders.resolverPath: resolveProvider('kling', config, poisonedAdapter, { allowLegacyBridge: true }) -> createNewAPIProvider() compatible native path
sunsetProviders.nativeSupportStatus: Migrated to native-compatible AI SDK resolution through createNewAPIProvider() while preserving provider type metadata as kling.
migrationConditions: Resolver poison-bridge test proves createLegacyBridgeProvider() is not used for kling.
Image/video endpoint, provider options, async status, failure normalization, and result normalization remain covered before deleting the OpenAICompatMediaAdapter builtin.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Keep out of AI_SDK_LEGACY_BRIDGE_MIGRATION_PROVIDER_TYPES; delete this sunset row when Kling contract tests fully cover the native-compatible provider path.

entries.surface: runtime fallback, provider degradation, media routing fallback, and retry behavior
entries.replacement: N/A: resilience behavior, not cleanup target
entries.removeAfter: Only remove a fallback path when tests prove the canonical path covers the legacy input or failure mode.
entries.notes: Text matches on fallback are not enough to classify code as dead or legacy.

entries.surface: Agent remaining legacy runtime compatibility paths: workflow usage recorder, artifact restore legacy index, document read legacy wording, capability registry legacy names, and IDC runtime string feedback guidance

tests: rg -n "createLegacyWorkflowUsageRecorder|AgentLegacyWorkflow|readArtifactRestoreEntriesWithLegacyFallback|toLegacyArtifactIndexPath|legacyToolNames|normalizeLegacyResourceRef|legacyContent|content preserves legacy full read" packages/neko-agent/packages/agent packages/neko-agent/packages/agent-types packages/neko-agent/packages/extension
entries.notes: The remaining Agent legacy/fallback words are separately classified as current bridge, runtime resilience, boundary canonicalizer, resource-cache compatibility, or test fixtures.

tests: rg -n "skillInjectionModule\\?:|legacy direct|legacy path|legacy setSection|legacy fallback|byte-for-byte identical|byte-identical" packages/neko-agent/packages/agent/src/skill packages/neko-agent/packages/agent/src/prompt packages/neko-agent/packages/agent/src/session
entries.notes: This was a duplicate implementation path, not a runtime resilience fallback.

entries.surface: Unwired .hook/<name>/HOOK.ts|js directory hook runtime: HookLoader, HookRuntimeManager, hook runtime compiler/sandbox, Extension HookManager, and hookSource bridge
entries.replacement: Current hook path: .neko/settings.json SettingsHookLoader for shell execution.
entries.removeAfter: Removed after repo scan found no production createHookManager/new HookManager/createProjectHookRuntimeManager/new HookLoader callers and Extension config only exposed an optional unused bridge.
entries.notes: This does not remove current ExecutorHooks or SettingsHookLoader support.

entries.surface: Superseded IDC projected task payload and creation-task projection runtime
entries.replacement: Ordinary TaskManager task state plus bounded conversation TODO projection; neither carries IDC run or stage identity.
entries.removeAfter: Removed by retire-idc-and-align-agent-creative-planning after ordinary TaskManager and TODO projection tests replaced the creation-task compatibility path.
entries.notes: The retained TaskManager owns real asynchronous execution only. TODO remains disposable display state and cannot recreate creation-task runtime state.

entries.surface: packages/neko-agent/packages/agent/src/subagent SubAgentConfig.allowedTools / SpecializedAgentPreset.allowedTools compatibility entrypoint and empty-array allow-all semantics
entries.replacement: Explicit AgentToolPolicy on SubAgentConfig and SpecializedAgentPreset.
entries.removeAfter: Removed after all SubAgent presets and tests migrated to explicit toolPolicy none/all/allow-list semantics; Skill.allowedTools remains a separate current skill-injection contract.
tests: rg -n 'allowedTools\\??:|allowedTools:|preset\\.allowedTools|config\\.allowedTools|legacy allowedTools|legacy empty allowedTools|Empty means all tools|Has preset allowedTools' packages/neko-agent/packages/agent/src/subagent packages/neko-skills/src/subagent -g'*.ts'
entries.notes: This does not remove Skill.allowedTools, injected capability allowedTools, or tool-guard allow-lists, which are current skill/capability contracts outside the SubAgent configuration surface.

entries.surface: Agent prompt and retired fixed-stage migration wording, StageMode/StageTaskShape contracts, and stage-planner activation path

entries.notes: Retained matches are limited to explicit poison fixtures and historical documentation; production runtime does not expose a stage success path.

entries.surface: .neko/hooks/*.md HookFile catalog, HookFileService scanner, HookSyncHandler bridge, and HookConfigSyncRuntime projection
entries.replacement: .neko/settings.json SettingsHookLoader and ConfigBridgeRuntime getConfigState() projection.
entries.removeAfter: Removed after repo scan confirmed HookFile catalog was not an execution path, Webview protocol had no ConfiguredHook consumer, and current hook execution already uses .neko/settings.json SettingsHookLoader.
entries.notes: The deleted catalog only read markdown hook descriptors for a configuration surface; it did not execute hooks. Runtime hook behavior remains covered by SettingsHookLoader and permission hook tests.

entries.surface: RetryHooks model fallback state and methods: modelFallback options, currentModel, tryFallback(), resetFallback(), and onModelSwitch callback
entries.replacement: Current RetryHooks tool retry path; provider/model routing remains owned by provider-router and media/service selection layers.
entries.removeAfter: Removed after repo scan confirmed AgentExecutor and hook runner never call RetryHooks.tryFallback(), while createExecutorHooks only configures toolRetryPolicy for transient tool failures.
entries.notes: This does not remove RetryHooks itself or runtime provider/model fallback contracts outside the executor hook chain.

entries.surface: CharacterDialogueController default transcript evaluator prompt projection, evaluator response parsing, and fallback report generation
entries.replacement: @neko/agent/runtime evaluateCharacterDialogueTranscript() with Extension supplying only Platform IService and selected model adapters.
entries.removeAfter: Migrated after boundary review confirmed transcript evaluation is dialogue-domain logic, while Extension should only bridge VSCode/Webview/Platform dependencies.
entries.notes: Runtime fallback evaluation remains current resilience; the cleanup removes Extension-owned domain fallback generation.

entries.surface: Semantic coverage tool planning.fallback normal-tool-analysis contract

entries.surface: Skill requiredSubpackages optional dependency fallback.message degradation contract

entries.surface: Unwired ApprovalEngine channel adapters: permission, quality-gate, and plan-review adapter wrappers plus adapter-only tests
entries.replacement: Current AgentSession._resolveToolConfirmation() permission approval path, core ApprovalEngine, and strategy packs. Quality review remains evidence/tool driven until a real pipeline gate is reintroduced.
entries.removeAfter: Removed after repo scan showed the adapter factories were only exported from approval/index.ts and used by their own tests; current production permission approval is in AgentSession and no pipeline qualityGate or plan-review caller exists.
entries.notes: This is not a runtime fallback removal. It deletes uncalled bridge wrappers after the current approval path was verified in AgentSession.
